ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn URI exists for Mutter Paneer specifically; mapped to the broader 'curry dish' class as the closest defensible FoodOn anchor. A more specific term for paneer-based curry is not available in the current FoodOn release.
ontology.foodex2_description: Dishes based on legumes and cheese — vegetable and cheese curry
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not contain a specific code for Mutter Paneer. Code A0CKP (composite dish, vegetable-based with dairy) is used as the closest defensible broader match.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept found for Mutter Paneer or equivalent Indian cottage cheese and pea curry as of 2026.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of Mutter Paneer is approximately 250 g, equivalent to a medium restaurant or home-cooked portion typically served alongside roti, naan, or jeera rice.

nutrient_source.title: Nutritive Value of Indian Foods
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values for Mutter Paneer are adapted from ICMR-NIN 'Nutritive Value of Indian Foods' using component data for paneer, green peas, tomato-onion gravy base, and cream/yogurt in proportions consistent with a standard North Indian preparation. Values for saturated fat are estimated from USDA FoodData Central (SR Legacy) paneer and cream component data where ICMR-NIN does not report fatty acid fractions separately.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — peer-reviewed analogous estimate for paneer and legume-based curry
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for Mutter Paneer exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 35 is estimated by analogy with mixed legume and dairy-based Indian curries (GI range 30–40) reported in Atkinson et al. 2021 and supporting peer-reviewed literature. The high protein and fat content from paneer and cream, combined with dietary fibre from green peas, substantially attenuates postprandial glucose response. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (35 × 17.0) / 100 = 5.95, where available carbohydrate per 250 g serving = 6.8 × 2.5 = 17.0 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (240 mg/100 g) and saturated fat from paneer and cream
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to 150–200 g and request reduced-salt preparation; substituting low-fat paneer and omitting added cream can reduce both sodium and saturated fat load meaningfully.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (~35) driven by high protein from paneer and dietary fibre from green peas
type2_diabetes.guidance: Mutter Paneer is suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed in a standard 200–250 g portion; the low GI and moderate protein content support glycaemic control, but pairing with refined-flour breads (naan, paratha) should be minimised to avoid elevating the overall meal glycaemic load.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (~5.8 g/100 g) from full-fat paneer and cream contributes to LDL-raising potential
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should limit Mutter Paneer to occasional consumption (≤2 servings per week), use low-fat paneer, replace cream with low-fat yogurt, and monitor total saturated fat intake against the daily limit of <7% of total energy.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Dairy (paneer, cream) and nightshades (tomatoes) may act as inflammatory triggers in susceptible individuals
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should monitor personal tolerance to dairy and tomato-based preparations; anti-inflammatory spices such as ginger, turmeric, and cumin present in the dish may offer minor offsetting benefits.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Mutter Paneer is a classic North Indian vegetarian curry consisting of soft cubes of paneer (fresh Indian cottage cheese) and sweet green peas (mutter) cooked in a rich, spiced tomato-onion gravy. The dish is prepared by sautéing onions, ginger, and garlic until golden, then adding tomatoes and cooking them down into a thick, jammy base seasoned with turmeric, coriander powder, cumin, and garam masala. Cream or whisked yogurt is stirred in at the end to create a smooth, velvety texture and to balance the acidity of the tomatoes. The result is a mildly spiced, aromatic curry with a creamy, slightly tangy gravy. Nutritionally, Mutter Paneer provides approximately 145 kcal per 100 g, with 7.5 g protein, 10.2 g fat (of which ~5.8 g is saturated), and 6.8 g carbohydrate. It is a good source of calcium (150 mg/100 g) from paneer, vitamin C (12 mg/100 g) from tomatoes and peas, and vitamin B12 (0.8 mcg/100 g) from dairy. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 35 (low), making it suitable for blood sugar management when paired with low-GI accompaniments. It is one of the most widely consumed vegetarian main dishes across India and is commonly served with roti, naan, or jeera rice. The dish is suitable for vegetarians and is naturally gluten-free. Clinically, it is appropriate for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to its low GI profile, but sodium and saturated fat content warrant moderation for those managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from ICMR-NIN component data for paneer, green peas, and tomato-onion gravy rather than a direct published composite analysis of Mutter Paneer as a finished dish. Saturated fat and fatty acid fractions are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy paneer and cream data. GI is an analogous estimate from peer-reviewed literature; no direct GI measurement for Mutter Paneer is available in the University of Sydney GI Database.
notes: 1. water_g is set to -1: moisture content for the composite Mutter Paneer dish as prepared is not directly reported in ICMR-NIN 'Nutritive Value of Indian Foods' or USDA FoodData Central for this specific preparation; estimation from components was not performed to avoid compounding uncertainty. 2. Saturated fat (5.8 g/100 g), monounsaturated_fat_g (2.9 g/100 g), polyunsaturated_fat_g (0.7 g/100 g), trans_fat_g (0.1 g/100 g), and cholesterol_mg (28.0 mg/100 g) are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for paneer and cream components in standard proportions, as ICMR-NIN does not report fatty acid fractions separately for this dish. 3. GI of 35 is an analogous estimate based on mixed legume and dairy-based Indian curry data from Atkinson et al. 2021 (Diabetes Care); no direct GI measurement for Mutter Paneer exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. 4. The input record contained three identical duplicate portion entries (all '1 serving, 250 g, is_default: true'); deduplicated to a single 250 g default serving. 5. The input health_suitability condition 'diabetes' has been mapped to the required key 'type2_diabetes' with human_condition_name 'Type 2 Diabetes' per Veritas scoring rules. 6. No core macro (protein_g, fat_g, carbohydrate_g) is zero; zero-macro justification rule does not apply.
